title: [InstCombine] Canonicalize more geps with constant gep bases and constant offsets.
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by extracting a reusable predicate (`shouldCanonicalizeGEPToPtrAdd`) that identifies when a GEP should be canonicalized to a `ptr.add`. The original pattern handled only scalable types or single-index GEPs with a multiply/shift. The generalization adds a new case: when the base pointer is another GEP with all-constant indices and the outer GEP has at least one non-zero constant index. This merges constant offsets from nested GEPs, enabling better constant folding.

The test cases validate this: `x12` and `x1y` show nested GEPs with constant indices being folded; `notDivisible` ensures alignment constraints are respected; `partialConstant2/3` test mixed constant/variable indices. The key insight is to separate pattern detection from transformation logic, making the optimization modular and extensible for future cases. Developers should identify common structural properties (like nested constant-offset GEPs) and encapsulate them in dedicated predicates.
